stories.headline: Parliamentary watchdog launches inquiry into Nigel Farage over undeclared £5m gift

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Reform spokesman: “Mr Farage’s office is in communications with the Parliamentary Commissioner for Standards. "He has always been clear that this was a personal, unconditional gift and no rules were broken. "We look forward to this being put to bed once and for all.”

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Farage says the gift was unconditional, received in 2024, and legally assessed as non-declarable. Whether he is right or wrong on the technicality, the enthusiasm for this investigation from a system that ignored grooming gangs, record small boat arrivals and ministerial conflicts for years tells its own story.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: #bbcqt That audience member is right. Reform UK Ltd are a party of billionaires who slip Nigel Farage 5 million pound bungs, and try to hide it from parliamentary authorities. Farage is a shyster. https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/may/13/nigel-farage-inquiry-gift-crypto-billionaire-reform-uk-christopher-harborne

stories.headline: US President Donald Trump holds high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing discussing trade, Taiwan, and Iran.

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: 😂NYT is in full meltdown mode: “Where Are The Women at the Trump-Xi Summit?!” Top brass from America, China, and the world’s biggest CEOs sit down… and it’s basically a sausage fest on both sides. The few women on the U.S. team? Stuck in communications roles. We’re watching two nuclear powers and the global economy try to hash out serious deals, and the hottest take is still “not enough ladies at the big boy table.”

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: 🚨 BREAKING: Trump and Xi meet in a closely watched summit, where they're expected to discuss the Iran war, tariffs and more. Follow our live blog for the latest news and analysis: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/live-blog/2026-05-13/trump-xi-us-china-summit

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Talks between US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping have been largely positive, though the future of Taiwan threatens to strain the relationship. stories.headline: El Niño emerging faster than expected, potentially becoming a historic Super El Niño by fall.
stories.body: El Niño is emerging even faster than expected in the Pacific Ocean and odds are increasing that it could become historically strong — a rare "Super" El Niño — by fall or winter. https://www.cnn.com/2026/05/14/weather/super-el-nino-climate?Date=20260514&Profile=CNN&utm_content=1778765068&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter

stories.headline: Parents in Pennsylvania school district petition to opt children out of using digital devices in curriculum.
stories.body: Kids in Lower Merion school district in Pennsylvania get iPads starting in kindergarten, switch to Chromebooks in second grade and get their own MacBooks in eighth grade. Hundreds of parents signed a petition asking to preserve their ability to opt their children out of using digital devices during the school day. The school district has pushed back, saying it’s not feasible to let hundreds of students opt out of technology that is essential to the curriculum.

stories.headline: Joe Rogan voices concern for Theo Von's well-being amid mental health struggles discussed on podcast.
stories.body:
Joe Rogan just took time out of his podcast to express genuine concern for his friend Theo Von.

He admitted that some of Von’s recent behavior after getting on SSRIs “freaks me out” — especially his comments about suicide.

ROGAN: “Theo Von’s going through the exact same thing and last time he was on the podcast he was explaining it to me.”

“It freaks me out because I know Theo has had conversations before...like even publicly.”

“He had a Netflix taping and it didn’t go well. It was like they actually never...they shelved it. They never used it.”

“And you know there was all these stories from people that were there saying he bombed. I think he just had a kind of a breakdown.”

“And when he was talking to the crowd and there’s a video of it, he said, you know, the people were saying, hey, we still love you.”

“He goes, thank you. Look, I’m just I’m trying not to take my own life.”

“And like you hear stuff like that and you just go like, oh, Jesus Christ.”

“I’ve known too many people that I didn’t think were going to kill themselves and then did.”

“And then he goes down these spirals where he starts talking about world events and freaking out. I’m like, oh, Jesus Christ! Like, I got to help this dude.”

stories.headline: Joe Rogan challenges Palantir CEO Alex Karp's support for mandatory national service post-Iran war.
stories.body:
ALERT: Joe Rogan says “F*ck you, you go” to Palantir after CEO Alex Karp argued that national service should be mandatory.

“I don’t understand why anybody would wanna support that… That sounds crazy. Especially after this Iran war, where everybody’s like why the f*ck are we in Iran?”

“How ’bout let’s figure out a way to use your technology so there’s no more wars? Wouldn’t that be a better goal?”

stories.headline: Lex Fridman plans spontaneous global travel starting with AI discussions in China and hitchhiking in rural areas.
stories.body:
I'm traveling the world for a bit, starting with China but then hopping around the globe, anywhere. Open to any adventure. No plans, only a backpack. Hoping to meet & get to know humans from all walks of life. The pic is from a long hike on the Great Wall. For me, as a fan of history, this was an epic experience.

In China, first I'm visiting a few big cities & talking to engineers at the heart of China's AI revolution. After that, if feeling crazy enough, I'm hitchhiking (first time) across rural China for a few weeks. Hitchhiking because I think it's the best way to meet rural folks who I would otherwise never get the chance to meet. I hope to do the same in US and other places.

I have a request, if you have a travel recommendation, fill out the form(s) below if you feel like it. Or share with folks who might have advice about such travel.

Form 1 - travel recommendation:
If you can, recommend to me an interesting place I should visit anywhere in the world. For this, fill out form 1. Not touristy stuff, but something off the beaten path, that tourists may not know about, but is legendary. It could be as remote as meeting a herder in the mountains who is a local legend. Asia, Middle East, Europe, India, South/North America, Africa, Australia, anywhere. In China, I'm hoping to visit maybe Heibei, Shanxi, Shaanxi, Gansu, Sichuan, Yunnan, etc, so recommendations for spots to visit are helpful.

Form 2 - coffee:
If you want to grab a coffee with me anywhere in the world, fill out form 2 (please don't use form 1 for that).

Anyway, I hectically tossed stuff in backpack. Realizing I don't have a clear plan of any kind, which is probably the only way to do it. LFG.

Love you all ❤️
